problem_62.natural_language: A group of order `p^k` with `k > 0` has nontrivial center.
problem_62.lean:
theorem exists_ne_one_mem_center_of_card_prime_pow
    {G : Type*} [Group G] [Fintype G] {p k : Nat} (hp : Nat.Prime p)
    (hk : k > 0) (hcard : Fintype.card G = p ^ k) :
    ∃ g : G, g ≠ 1 ∧ g ∈ Subgroup.center G
problem_62.natural_language_proof:
Proof: apply the class equation:
`|G| = |Z(G)| + ∑ [G : C_G(x)]` over representatives of noncentral conjugacy classes. Each index
`[G : C_G(x)]` is the size of a conjugacy class and divides `|G| = p^k`, hence is a power of `p`
and in particular is divisible by `p` whenever the class is nontrivial. Since `k > 0`, the prime
`p` divides `|G|`. Therefore `|G| ≡ |Z(G)| (mod p)`, so `p` also divides `|Z(G)|`. It follows that
`Z(G)` has more than one element. Thus there exists `g ≠ 1` with `g ∈ Z(G)`.
